Tests that the "NEGATIVE_INFINITY" property does not exist in the Number object.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that iterates over the properties of the `Number` object in JavaScript. It checks if the property `NEGATIVE_INFINITY` exists within the `Number` object. If the property is found, the script calls the `assert` function with `false`, which typically indicates a failed assertion in testing contexts. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on verifying the absence of the `NEGATIVE_INFINITY` property in the `Number` object.
